data.title: Ballistic Trajectory
data.statement:
A cannonball is fired from the ground with an initial speed [[V0]] at an angle [[theta]].

The horizontal and vertical motions are independent :
- <<Horizontal>> = constant velocity (no acceleration)
- <<Vertical>> = constant acceleration (gravity only)
- g = {{9.80665}} m/s²

The <<horizontal range>> [[R]] is the distance traveled by the projectile when it hits the ground ([[y]] = 0, [[t]] > 0) :
[[R]] = [[V0x]] * [[T]]
where [[V0x]] = [[V0]] * cos([[theta]]) and [[T]] is the total flight time

A target is placed at a horizontal distance [[D]] from the launch point.

Given [[V0]], [[theta]] and [[D]], determine if the projectile lands within <<0.5m>> (Inclusive) of the target.
Print {{HIT}} or {{MISS}} [[error]] where [[error]] = [[R]] - [[D]].
rounded to <<2 decimal places>> (signed).

You can check more about trajectory here: https://byjus.com/trajectory-formula/
data.stubGenerator:
read v0:int theta:int d:float
write HIT
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1 :>> Three space-separated values : [[V0]] [[theta]] [[D]]
- [[V0]] = initial speed (integer, m/s)
- [[theta]] = launch angle (integer, degrees)
- [[D]] = target distance (float, meters)
data.outputDescription:
If the projectile lands within <<0.5m>> (Inclusive) of the target :
{{HIT}}

Otherwise :
{{MISS}} [[error]]
where [[error]] = [[R]] - [[D]] rounded to 2 decimal places (signed)

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

A cannonball is fired from the ground with an initial speed V0 at an angle theta.

The horizontal and vertical motions are independent :
- Horizontal = constant velocity (no acceleration)
- Vertical = constant acceleration (gravity only)
- g = 9.80665 m/s²

The horizontal range R is the distance traveled by the projectile when it hits the ground (y = 0, t > 0) :
R = V0x * T
where V0x = V0 * cos(theta) and T is the total flight time

A target is placed at a horizontal distance D from the launch point.

Given V0, theta and D, determine if the projectile lands within 0.5m (Inclusive) of the target.
Print HIT or MISS error where error = R - D.
rounded to 2 decimal places (signed).

You can check more about trajectory here: https://byjus.com/trajectory-formula/
Input
Line 1 : Three space-separated values : V0 theta D
- V0 = initial speed (integer, m/s)
- theta = launch angle (integer, degrees)
- D = target distance (float, meters)
Output
If the projectile lands within 0.5m (Inclusive) of the target :
HIT

Otherwise :
MISS error
where error = R - D rounded to 2 decimal places (signed)
Constraints
1 ≤ V0 ≤ 100
1 ≤ theta ≤ 89
1 ≤ D ≤ 1000
Example
Input
50 45 254.93
Output
HIT
